title: rubus pseudodoftanensis
intro: Rubus pseudodoftanensis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus pseudodoftanensis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including diarrhea and sore throats.
